senses.definition: in a way that relates to behavior or observable actions rather than thoughts or feelings
examples: The study measured the participants' responses behaviourally rather than asking them directly.
Behaviourally, the child showed signs of anxiety in unfamiliar situations.

senses.definition: a theory that focuses on observable actions and responses rather than thoughts or feelings
examples: Behaviourism became influential in psychology during the early 20th century.
The teacher used behaviourism to shape students' study habits.

senses.definition: plural of behaviourism; multiple theories or instances within the behaviourist framework
examples: The behaviourisms proposed by the researchers varied in emphasis on reinforcement.
Different behaviourisms have influenced education and therapy.

senses.definition: a person who believes in or studies behaviourism
examples: The behaviourist argued that all learning is a change in behavior.
She trained as a behaviourist and now works in clinical psychology.

senses.definition: relating to or characteristic of behaviourism or its adherents
examples: Behaviourist methods focus on observable outcomes.
A behaviourist approach was adopted in the study.

senses.definition: relating to behaviourism or its methods
examples: The experiment followed a behaviouristic framework.
Behaviouristic explanations emphasize observable actions.

senses.definition: plural of behaviourist; people who adhere to or study behaviourism
examples: Behaviourists often focus on reinforcement in learning processes.
Several well-known behaviourists contributed to early psychology.

senses.definition: the actions or responses of a person or animal
examples: Good behaviours at school are encouraged by rewards.
Animal behaviours can be studied in natural habitats.

senses.definition: to cut off the head of a person or animal
examples: The knight was beheaded in the medieval tale.
The tale describes how the warrior beheads his foe.

senses.definition: to cut off the top or leading part of something, metaphorically or literally
examples: The article beheaded the list of topics.
The company decided to behead the project because of budget constraints.

senses.definition: the act or instance of beheading
examples: Beheadal is rarely used in modern English.
Historical accounts sometimes describe beheadals in royal executions.

senses.definition: plural form of beheadal; instances of beheading
examples: Historical records mention several beheadals.
Beheadals were common in certain ancient legal codes.

senses.definition: having had the head cut off; executed by beheading
examples: The beheaded statue stood as a grim reminder.
The king was beheaded after a long trial.

senses.definition: a person who beheads or a tool used to behead, though rarely used today
examples: The beheader is mentioned in ancient legends, though the term is largely obsolete.
Historical texts refer to the beheader in certain practices.

senses.definition: plural of beheader; people who behead
examples: Legends mention several beheaders in the saga.
Beheaders are rarely discussed in modern texts.

senses.definition: the act of cutting off someone's head
examples: Beheading was a common punishment in some ancient laws.
The scene described a dramatic beheading.

senses.definition: present participle of behead; the act of cutting off the head
examples: The blade began beheading the figure.
They discussed the beheading of the statue in the tale.

senses.definition: to headline (a news story) with a beheading-related phrase or to remove leading items from a list, though very uncommon
examples: The article beheadlined the ancient punishment.
In some translations, passages are beheadlined to emphasize the headline.

senses.definition: third person singular of behead; cuts off the head
examples: The executioner beheads the traitor.
In the legend, the hero beheads the dragon.

senses.definition: to hear; to listen; archaic or dialectal form
examples: The old ballad says the prince would behear the call of the horn.
In some dialects, people say 'behear' instead of 'hear'.

senses.definition: third person singular of behear; hears
examples: He behears the distant sound.
The elder behears every word with careful attention.

senses.definition: to attend as a hearer; to listen
examples: She behearse the audience with sympathy.
The monk behearse the teachings with rapt attention.

senses.definition: to hedge about or surround; to place hedges around
examples: They behedge the garden with shrubs.
The walls behedge the property for privacy.

senses.definition: a proper noun or place name; not common in modern usage
examples: Beheira is a district in some historical maps.
The manuscript mentions Beheira in ancient texts.

senses.definition: past tense of behold; to see or observe something
examples: She beheld a stunning sunset over the sea.
They beheld the old city with awe.

senses.definition: to help; to assist; archaic or dialectal form
examples: They set out to behelp the villagers.
In old texts, characters would behelp their companions.

senses.definition: a very large and powerful thing or creature
examples: The new data center is a behemoth of technology.
The project turned into a behemoth to manage.
